name: build
context: Build Docker images from Dockerfiles with BuildKit, layer caching, and build arguments
info: This sub-skill covers writing Dockerfiles, building images with `docker build` or `docker buildx`, leveraging layer caching for fast rebuilds, and parameterizing builds with build arguments. BuildKit is the modern build backend that enables parallel stage execution, secret mounts, SSH forwarding, and advanced caching strategies. Each Dockerfile instruction (FROM, RUN, COPY, etc.) creates a cached layer. Optimizing layer order and using .dockerignore are critical for build performance. Build arguments parameterize builds at build time without hardcoding values. The target flag selects a specific stage in a multi-stage Dockerfile. This sub-skill focuses on the build step itself; see [[docker/images/push]] for distribution and [[docker/images/multi-stage]] for multi-stage optimization patterns.
triggers: write a dockerfile for my app
body:
# Building Docker Images

Building a Docker image involves writing a Dockerfile that defines the image layers, then running `docker build` to execute the instructions and produce a tagged image. Each instruction (`FROM`, `RUN`, `COPY`, etc.) creates a layer that is cached and reused on subsequent builds if the inputs have not changed.

## Dockerfile Syntax

```dockerfile
# Base image
FROM node:20-alpine AS base

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Copy dependency manifests first (cache-friendly)
COPY package.json package-lock.json ./

# Install dependencies
RUN npm ci --production

# Copy application source
COPY . .

# Expose port
EXPOSE 3000

# Health check
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=3s --retries=3 \
  CMD curl -f http://localhost:3000/health || exit 1

# Non-root user
USER node

# Start command
CMD ["node", "server.js"]
```

### Key Instructions

| Instruction | Purpose | Layer? |
|---|---|---|
| `FROM` | Set base image; starts a new build stage | Yes |
| `WORKDIR` | Set working directory for subsequent instructions | Yes |
| `COPY` | Copy files from build context into the image | Yes |
| `ADD` | Like COPY but supports URLs and auto-extraction of archives | Yes |
| `RUN` | Execute a command and commit the resulting layer | Yes |
| `ENV` | Set environment variable persisted at runtime | Yes |
| `ARG` | Define build-time variable (not persisted in image) | No |
| `EXPOSE` | Document the port the container listens on | No |
| `VOLUME` | Declare a mount point for external volumes | Yes |
| `USER` | Set the user for subsequent instructions and runtime | Yes |
| `CMD` | Default command when container starts (overridable) | No |
| `ENTRYPOINT` | Main executable (CMD becomes arguments to it) | No |
| `HEALTHCHECK` | Define how Docker checks container health | No |
| `LABEL` | Add metadata key-value pairs to the image | Yes |

## Build Commands

```bash
# Basic build with tag
docker build -t myapp:latest .

# Build with BuildKit (recommended)
DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 docker build -t myapp:latest .

# Build with buildx (BuildKit-native)
docker buildx build -t myapp:latest .

# Build with custom Dockerfile path
docker build -f docker/Dockerfile.production -t myapp:prod .

# Build specific stage in multi-stage Dockerfile
docker build --target builder -t myapp:builder .

# Build with no cache
docker build --no-cache -t myapp:latest .

# Build with progress output
docker build --progress=plain -t myapp:latest .
```

## Build Arguments

Build arguments parameterize the build without hardcoding values:

```dockerfile
ARG NODE_VERSION=20
FROM node:${NODE_VERSION}-alpine

ARG APP_ENV=production
ENV APP_ENV=${APP_ENV}

ARG BUILD_DATE
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.created=${BUILD_DATE}
```

```bash
docker build \
  --build-arg NODE_VERSION=18 \
  --build-arg APP_ENV=staging \
  --build-arg BUILD_DATE=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) \
  -t myapp:staging .
```

Never pass secrets as build args; they are visible in `docker history`. Use BuildKit secret mounts instead.

## Layer Caching

Docker caches each layer. If an instruction and its inputs have not changed, the cached layer is reused. Optimize by:

1. **Order layers from least to most frequently changed** -- base image and system packages first, application code last.
2. **Copy dependency manifests before source** -- `COPY package.json` before `COPY .` so the dependency install layer is cached when only source changes.
3. **Use `.dockerignore`** -- exclude `node_modules`, `.git`, `dist`, build artifacts, and anything not needed in the build context.
4. **Combine RUN commands** -- `RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y curl && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*` to reduce layers and keep the image clean.
5. **Leverage BuildKit cache mounts** -- `RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.npm npm ci` persists package manager caches across builds.

### .dockerignore

```
node_modules
.git
.gitignore
*.md
dist
coverage
.env*
.DS_Store
Dockerfile
docker-compose.yml
```

## BuildKit Features

Enable BuildKit with `DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1` or use `docker buildx`:

- **Parallel execution** -- independent stages build concurrently
- **Cache mounts** -- persist package manager caches across builds:
  ```dockerfile
  RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.npm npm ci
  RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/var/cache/apt apt-get install -y curl
  ```
- **Secret mounts** -- expose secrets without baking them into layers:
  ```dockerfile
  RUN --mount=type=secret,id=npmrc,target=/root/.npmrc npm ci
  ```
  ```bash
  docker build --secret id=npmrc,src=$HOME/.npmrc -t myapp .
  ```
- **SSH forwarding** -- clone private repos during build:
  ```dockerfile
  RUN --mount=type=ssh git clone git@github.com:org/private-lib.git
  ```
  ```bash
  docker build --ssh default -t myapp .
  ```
- **Build cache export/import** -- share caches across CI runners:
  ```bash
  docker buildx build --cache-to type=registry,ref=myapp:cache --cache-from type=registry,ref=myapp:cache -t myapp .
  ```

## Build Context

The build context is the set of files sent to the Docker daemon. Keep it small:

- Use `.dockerignore` to exclude unnecessary files
- Use a specific directory: `docker build -f Dockerfile path/to/context`
- For remote contexts: `docker build https://github.com/org/repo.git#main`
- BuildKit supports stdin Dockerfile: `docker build -f- . <<'EOF'\nFROM alpine\nRUN echo hello\nEOF`

sections.title: Dockerfile syntax
sections.body: A Dockerfile is a text file with instructions: FROM sets the base image, WORKDIR sets the working directory, COPY and ADD bring files into the image, RUN executes commands, EXPOSE documents ports, ENV sets environment variables, ARG defines build-time variables, CMD and ENTRYPOINT define the default command, USER sets the runtime user, HEALTHCHECK defines health monitoring. Each layer-creating instruction is cached independently.

sections.title: Build arguments
sections.body: Define with ARG name=default in the Dockerfile, pass with --build-arg name=value at build time. Build args are available during the build but not at runtime (use ENV to persist). Common uses: base image version, build environment, feature flags, build metadata labels. Never pass secrets as build args -- they are visible in image history. Use BuildKit secret mounts instead.

sections.title: Layer caching strategies
sections.body: Docker caches each layer and reuses it if the instruction and inputs are unchanged. Order instructions from least to most frequently changed. Copy dependency manifests before source code. Use .dockerignore to exclude files that cause unnecessary cache invalidation. BuildKit cache mounts (--mount=type=cache) persist package manager caches across builds. Use --no-cache to force a full rebuild when needed.

sections.title: BuildKit features
sections.body: BuildKit provides parallel stage execution, cache mounts for package managers, secret mounts that don't leak into layers, SSH forwarding for private repo access during builds, and build cache export/import for sharing across CI runners. Enable with DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 or use docker buildx. BuildKit is the default build backend in Docker 23.0+.

sections.title: Build context
sections.body: The build context is the set of files sent to the Docker daemon for the build. Keep it small with .dockerignore. Specify custom context paths, use remote git URLs, or pipe Dockerfiles via stdin. BuildKit transfers only the files referenced by COPY/ADD instructions, not the entire context directory.

scripts.name: Build Image
scripts.description: Build a Docker image from a Dockerfile with optional build arguments and target stage
scripts.inline: DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 docker build ${DOCKERFILE_PATH:+-f "$DOCKERFILE_PATH"} ${TARGET:+--target "$TARGET"} ${BUILD_ARGS:+$(echo "$BUILD_ARGS" | tr ',' '\n' | sed 's/^/--build-arg /')} ${NO_CACHE:+--no-cache} --progress=plain -t "${DOCKER_IMAGE}:${DOCKER_TAG}" "${BUILD_CONTEXT:-.}" && echo '' && echo "Successfully built ${DOCKER_IMAGE}:${DOCKER_TAG}" && docker images "${DOCKER_IMAGE}:${DOCKER_TAG}" --format 'Size: {{.Size}}'

NO_CACHE.description: Set to any value to disable layer cache

scripts.name: Build with Secrets
scripts.description: Build a Docker image using BuildKit secret mounts to avoid leaking credentials into image layers
scripts.inline: DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 docker build --secret id="${SECRET_ID}",src="${SECRET_FILE}" ${DOCKERFILE_PATH:+-f "$DOCKERFILE_PATH"} -t "${DOCKER_IMAGE}:${DOCKER_TAG}" "${BUILD_CONTEXT:-.}" && echo '' && echo "Built ${DOCKER_IMAGE}:${DOCKER_TAG} with secret mount '${SECRET_ID}'"

SECRET_ID.description: Secret identifier used in Dockerfile RUN --mount=type=secret,id=<this>

SECRET_FILE.description: Path to the secret file on the host

scripts.name: Build with Cache Export
scripts.description: Build an image and export the build cache to a registry for sharing across CI runners
scripts.inline: docker buildx build --cache-from type=registry,ref="${CACHE_IMAGE}:cache" --cache-to type=registry,ref="${CACHE_IMAGE}:cache",mode=max ${DOCKERFILE_PATH:+-f "$DOCKERFILE_PATH"} -t "${DOCKER_IMAGE}:${DOCKER_TAG}" --push "${BUILD_CONTEXT:-.}" && echo '' && echo "Built and pushed ${DOCKER_IMAGE}:${DOCKER_TAG} with cache at ${CACHE_IMAGE}:cache"

DOCKER_IMAGE.description: Image repository name with registry prefix

CACHE_IMAGE.description: Registry image used for build cache (e.g. ghcr.io/org/myapp)

target.description: Target stage name in multi-stage Dockerfile

noCache.description: Disable layer cache for this build

imageId.description: SHA256 digest of the built image

layers.description: Number of layers in the built image

cached.description: Whether the build used cached layers

depends.reason: Docker Engine must be installed and running to build images
Parent skill for image lifecycle management

author: dojo-community
